title: WEATHERIZATION ASSISTANCE PROGRAM - 211info
text: COMMUNITY IN ACTION WEATHERIZATION ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides home health and safety modifications designed to lower the cost of home heating. Modifications may include duct sealing, ceiling and wall insulation, fan replacement, window and door replacement, or addressing additional issues found in the home after inspection. 915 SW 3rd Avenue Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Household income at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm Email (541) 889-9555 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 889-9555 Main phone Client Services (541) 889-1060 Administration (541) 889-0768 FAX Intake (541) 889-9555 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information. Client must fill out an intake form to determine eligibility. Wait list is prioritized by clients who are 60 or older, who have verified disabilities, or families with children 18 years and younger. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: -Identification for all adults age18 and older -Social Security cards for all household members -Current proof of income for the most recent 30 days; Social Security recipients MUST bring your social security benefit letter -Most recent energy bill with the applicant’s name -Declaration of Household Income form when applicable LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Harney and Malheur Counties TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located at the base of St. Alphonsus Medical Center, across from the Lion's Park, and across the parking lot from the Ontario Chamber of Commerce building. Services: Weatherization Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY HOUSING/SHELTER - HOUSING RESOURCE CENTER HOUSING/SHELTER - READY TO RENT HOUSING/SHELTER - RENT ASSISTANCE HOUSING/SHELTER - RESIDENTIAL REHABILITATION REVOLVING LOAN AND GRANT PROGRAMS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES UTILITIES - UTILITY ASSISTANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. COMMUNITY IN ACTION
